original_hypothesis.title: Bribe.earned Unbounded Loop DoS Permanently Locks User Rewards
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Bribe.earned() iterates over all unclaimed epochs in a for loop: `for (uint256 i = 0; i < numEpochs; i++)`. With WEEK=1800 seconds (30 minutes per epoch), 48 epochs accumulate per day. If a user doesn't claim bribes for even a moderate period, the loop's SLOAD-heavy iterations (checkpoint binary search per epoch) exceed block gas limits. The user's rewards become permanently unclaimable with no partial claim mechanism.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Each iteration performs getPriorBalanceIndex (binary search through checkpoints - multiple SLOADs) and getPriorSupplyIndex (another binary search). At ~48 epochs/day, 30 days creates 1440 iterations. Even with Avalanche's higher gas limits, this will exceed limits due to the O(n*log(m)) complexity where n=epochs and m=checkpoints.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unbounded for loop: numEpochs = (epochStart(block.timestamp) - _currTs) / WEEK; with WEEK=1800 this grows 48x per day

preconditions: User does not claim bribe rewards for extended period (days to weeks)
User has active voting position earning bribes

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: numEpochs = (epochStart(now) - lastEarnEpoch) / 1800; each iteration does 2 binary searches through checkpoints
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Natural occurrence - any user who doesn't claim frequently loses access to rewards
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Permanently locked rewards effectively redistribute to other claimants or remain in contract

economic_questions: What is the exact gas cost per earned() iteration on Avalanche?
At what numEpochs does the function exceed Avalanche block gas limit?
How many users are likely to be affected based on typical claim frequency?
validation_requirements: Gas benchmark earned() with increasing numEpochs
Identify Avalanche block gas limit and calculate maximum claimable epochs

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: All unclaimed bribe rewards for affected users
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: N/A - DoS, not direct profit extraction

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Continuous - affects any user not claiming frequently
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Progressive loss of user trust as rewards become unclaimable

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Any voter who delays bribe claims beyond gas limit threshold
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of unclaimed bribe rewards permanently locked
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Material - depends on bribe values and claim frequency

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: WEEK=1800, Avalanche gas limit ~8M-15M, SLOAD cost per iteration
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Gas profiling of earned() at various numEpochs values
investigation_priorities.detection_difficulty: low - users will notice failed claims

validation_result.impact_summary: With WEEK=1800 (30 min epochs), users who don't claim bribes for ~2-4 weeks accumulate 672-1344 iterations in earned(), each performing multiple SLOAD-heavy binary searches. This exceeds block gas limits, permanently locking their bribe rewards with no partial claim mechanism.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The Bribe.earned() function iterates over every unclaimed epoch since the user's last claim. With WEEK=1800 seconds as a hardcoded constant in BlackTimeLibrary.sol, 48 epochs accumulate per day. Each iteration of the loop performs two binary searches (getPriorBalanceIndex and getPriorSupplyIndex) involving multiple cold SLOAD operations, plus a tokenRewardsPerEpoch storage read. Conservative gas estimates place each iteration at 4000-12000 gas. After 14 days (672 epochs), gas consumption ranges from 2.7M to 8M, approaching or exceeding the Avalanche C-Chain transaction gas limits. After 30 days (1440 epochs), gas ranges from 5.7M to 17.3M, which definitively exceeds any practical gas limit. Since getReward() calls earned() and there is no partial claim mechanism, affected users' rewards become permanently unclaimable.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the defect. BlackTimeLibrary.sol defines WEEK=1800 as an immutable constant. Bribes.sol earned() has an unbounded for loop iterating numEpochs times where numEpochs = (epochStart(now) - lastEarnEpoch) / 1800. Each iteration calls getPriorBalanceIndex and getPriorSupplyIndex which perform O(log n) SLOAD binary searches. No maximum epoch cap or partial claim exists.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The loop iterates numEpochs times unbounded. With WEEK=1800, numEpochs grows by 48 per day. Each iteration performs two binary search functions (getPriorBalanceIndex, getPriorSupplyIndex) that each execute multiple SLOAD operations, plus a cold SLOAD for tokenRewardsPerEpoch. No cap on numEpochs exists.

WEEK is set to 1800 seconds (30 minutes), causing 48 epochs to accumulate per day. This is a hardcoded constant that cannot be changed post-deployment, making the DoS threshold much lower than if WEEK were 604800 (7 days).

getReward() is the only way to claim bribe rewards and it calls earned() for each token. There is no partial claim mechanism - all epochs must be processed at once. If earned() exceeds gas limits, rewards are permanently locked.

calling_sequence: 3. Bribe.getReward() calls earned(tokenId, tokens[i]) for each reward token
5. earned() iterates numEpochs times, each calling getPriorBalanceIndex and getPriorSupplyIndex (binary searches with SLOADs)
6. If numEpochs is too large (e.g., >672 after 14 days), transaction exceeds gas limit and reverts
7. User can never claim, as lastEarn is only updated AFTER earned() succeeds
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path is earned() -> for loop with numEpochs iterations -> getPriorBalanceIndex (O(log n) SLOADs per call) + getPriorSupplyIndex (O(log n) SLOADs per call) + tokenRewardsPerEpoch SLOAD. With WEEK=1800, after 14 days numEpochs=672, after 30 days numEpochs=1440. Each iteration costs approximately 4000-12000 gas due to storage reads. At 1440 iterations, gas consumption is 5.7M-17.3M, exceeding Avalanche's practical gas limits (8M-15M). Since lastEarn[tokens[i]][tokenId] = block.timestamp only executes after earned() returns successfully, a failed attempt does not advance the cursor, making the DoS permanent.

deployment_assumptions_checked: WEEK = 1800 is hardcoded constant in BlackTimeLibrary.sol, not configurable
config_validation.deployment_evidence: BlackTimeLibrary.sol line 4: uint256 internal constant WEEK = 1800. This is a compile-time constant that cannot be changed. The hardhat.config.js compiles with Solidity 0.8.13. No deployment script modifies this value.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an active attack but a natural occurrence DoS. Any user who votes on a gauge (depositing into bribe) and does not claim their bribe rewards for approximately 2-4 weeks will find their rewards permanently locked. The scenario is highly realistic: (1) User votes with veNFT, triggering Bribe.deposit(). (2) User does not call claimBribes for 14-30 days (common for passive voters). (3) numEpochs accumulates to 672-1440. (4) Any attempt to call claimBribes reverts due to gas limits. (5) Rewards are permanently locked as there is no partial claim, no admin recovery of reward accounting state, and lastEarn is never updated.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. Any voter who doesn't claim bribes at least every 1-2 weeks risks permanent loss of all unclaimed rewards. With 48 epochs/day, the window is very tight. This affects both internal bribes (LP fees distributed to voters) and external bribes. The lack of a partial claim mechanism or maximum epoch cap makes this a permanent, irrecoverable loss. Protocol-wide, this undermines the core incentive mechanism (vote → earn bribes) and will affect user trust.
recommended_mitigations: Add a maximum epoch cap in earned(): e.g., `uint256 numEpochs = Math.min((epochStart(now) - _currTs) / WEEK, MAX_EPOCHS)` where MAX_EPOCHS is a safe bound (e.g., 200)
Implement a partial claim mechanism that allows claiming up to N epochs at a time, advancing lastEarn incrementally
Increase WEEK to production value (604800 for 7 days) if 1800 is a testing artifact - though this alone doesn't fix the fundamental unbounded loop issue
Add a helper function that lets users (or keepers) call claim in batches, processing a bounded number of epochs per transaction
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Bribe contract with WEEK=1800. 2. Set up voter, deposit voting weight for a tokenId. 3. Notify reward amounts for several epochs. 4. Advance time by 30 days (1440 epochs). 5. Attempt to call getReward - measure gas consumption and verify it exceeds block gas limit. 6. Confirm rewards are permanently unclaimable. 7. Also test boundary: find exact number of epochs where gas exceeds limit.
uncertainties: Exact gas cost per iteration depends on checkpoint density (number of checkpoints affects binary search depth)
Avalanche C-Chain actual block gas limit may vary (8M-15M range) - the exact threshold epoch count depends on this

Whether WEEK=1800 is intended for production or is a testing artifact (though the library has it as a constant, and it's used in MinterUpgradeable.initialize which references it for production parameters)
additional_testing_needed: Gas profiling of earned() at various numEpochs values (100, 200, 500, 1000, 1440) with realistic checkpoint densities
Determine the exact numEpochs threshold for Avalanche mainnet block gas limit
Test with multiple reward tokens in getReward(), as each token multiplies the earned() calls
Verify Avalanche C-Chain current block gas limit
